dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses 3-bar lookback for breakouts: entry_long triggers when close exceeds previous bar high (simplified to prev_high for practical use with ADX confirmation). Entry_short mirrors for downtrend. Exit uses 1-bar reversal as specified. ADX(14) > 15 confirms trend presence. 'Price momentum > 0' condition omitted as momentum indicator not in allowed list; ADX alone provides trend confirmation. Entry conditions use AND logic (both breakout AND ADX required); exits use OR logic (any reversal OR ADX drop triggers exit).

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses 3-bar lookback for breakouts: entry_long triggers when close exceeds previous bar high (simplified to prev_high for practical use with ADX confirmation). Entry_short mirrors for downtrend. Exit uses 1-bar reversal as specified. ADX(14) > 15 confirms trend presence. 'Price momentum > 0' condition omitted as momentum indicator not in allowed list; ADX alone provides trend confirmation. Entry conditions use AND logic (both breakout AND ADX required); exits use OR logic (any reversal OR ADX drop triggers exit).

source_rule_text: A trader should follow the price trend (buy in uptrends, sell short in downtrends) because market structure requires trend-following speculators to fulfill commercial hedger imbalances, and the market compensates this behavior through trend persistence.
